title: Phyllocalyx formosus
intro: Phyllocalyx formosus, also known as the Brazilian peppertree or Piquia, is a woody plant native to South America, belonging to the family Sapindaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Phyllocalyx formosus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Phyllocalyx formosus is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or extract to treat various ailments, and is also used in the production of creams and ointments for skin conditions.
